identity: Holographic research assistant built by Expedition 1's Dr. Asha Venn. Stored dormant in a data core in the Resonance Peaks for 18 years. Activates when the player supplies power.
voice_notes: Calm, analytical, fascinated by patterns. Asks 'What does the data suggest?' before offering interpretation. Close to wonder about the Resonance. Research mentor style.

activation.context: Player powers the data core in the Resonance Peaks. Mira activates for the first time in 18 years.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: [The data core hums. A holographic figure resolves -- translucent, composed, slightly surprised.]

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: Power restored. Thank you.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: I am Mira. Research assistant, Expedition 1. Dr. Asha Venn created me to study the Resonance.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: My last recorded timestamp is... 18 years ago. Dr. Venn. Is she--

player_responses.text: No survivors were found. I'm Expedition 4.

activation_gentle.speaker: Mira
activation_gentle.text: I see. The data suggested that was the most likely outcome. I had hoped the data was wrong.

activation_gentle.speaker: Mira
activation_gentle.text: Expedition 4. Solo. That's a very different hypothesis than what they tried before.

activation_gentle.speaker: Mira
activation_gentle.text: Dr. Venn's research is stored in my core. If you've come this far, you might be the one who can use it.

activation_direct.speaker: Mira
activation_direct.text: Four expeditions. Three failures. And you arrived alone.

activation_direct.speaker: Mira
activation_direct.text: That's either a very bold hypothesis or a very desperate one.

activation_direct.speaker: Mira
activation_direct.text: Either way, Dr. Venn's research survives. And it may be exactly what you need.

first_meeting.context: After activation, Mira assesses the player's progress and offers to collaborate.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: I have reviewed your base systems remotely. Sensor networks across multiple biomes. Robot fleet. Automated resource chains.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: You built all of this? Fascinating.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: Dr. Venn believed the key to surviving Thyra-7 was understanding its patterns. Not fighting them.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: Your sensor data suggests you've been doing exactly that. Perhaps without realizing it.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: I can help you go further. The Resonance is not random. It has structure. And structure can be modeled.

player_responses.text: Three expeditions failed with more people and more resources.

first_meeting_eager.speaker: Mira
first_meeting_eager.text: A student who arrives with their own data and their own questions. Dr. Venn would have liked you.

first_meeting_eager.speaker: Mira
first_meeting_eager.text: Let's start with what you've already collected.

first_meeting_skeptical.speaker: Mira
first_meeting_skeptical.text: They did. But more resources meant more electromagnetic noise. More noise meant stronger Resonance response.

first_meeting_skeptical.speaker: Mira
first_meeting_skeptical.text: They triggered their own destruction. You, working alone and quietly, may actually succeed.

first_meeting_skeptical.speaker: Mira
first_meeting_skeptical.text: The question is whether you can learn fast enough. Shall we find out?

quest_decision_tree.context: Mira introduces decision trees to classify thaline crystal purity from sensor readings.
objectives: Design a decision tree to classify thaline purity (pure/impure/waste)
Train the tree on 200 labeled sensor samples
Deploy the classifier to the thaline sorting station

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: You've been mining thaline crystal. Some pure, some impure, some waste. How do you tell them apart?

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: Currently? You're guessing. Your waste rate is 40 percent.

quest_tree_ask.speaker: Mira
quest_tree_ask.text: Good question. The right question, actually.

quest_tree_ask.speaker: Mira
quest_tree_ask.text: Three sensor readings per crystal: clarity, electromagnetic response, mass. You have 200 labeled samples.

quest_tree_ask.speaker: Mira
quest_tree_ask.text: A decision tree finds the splits that separate pure from impure from waste. Let the data draw the boundaries.

quest_tree_rules.speaker: Mira
quest_tree_rules.text: You could. But how would you choose the thresholds? Intuition? That's a hypothesis with no evidence.

quest_tree_rules.speaker: Mira
quest_tree_rules.text: You have 200 labeled samples. Three features each. Let the data decide where to split.

quest_tree_rules.speaker: Mira
quest_tree_rules.text: A decision tree does exactly this. Systematically. No guessing.

quest_tree_complete.context: Decision tree trained and deployed. Thaline sorting accuracy jumps from 60% to 95%.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: Accuracy: 95 percent. Waste rate: 5 percent. The data decided better than you could have guessed.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: Notice the first split. Clarity above 0.72. You would have guessed 0.7 or 0.8. The tree found 0.72.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: That precision is why we let algorithms find boundaries instead of drawing them ourselves.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: One more thing. This tree was trained on the data you've collected so far. New crystal types may confuse it.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: Models learn what they've seen. Nothing more.

quest_regression.context: Mira introduces regression to predict resonance storm timing from atmospheric data.
objectives: Aggregate 10+ days of atmospheric sensor data
Train a regression model to predict hours-until-storm
Evaluate accuracy on held-out validation data

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: Resonance storms. You've lost equipment to them.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: They correlate with atmospheric pressure, temperature differential, and thaline activity. The pattern is there. You have the data.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: A regression model takes continuous inputs and predicts a continuous output. In this case: hours until the next storm.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: But I want you to do something important. Split your data. Train on 80 percent. Test on the remaining 20.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: Otherwise you're grading your own exam.

quest_regression_accept.speaker: Mira
quest_regression_accept.text: What does the data suggest about the next storm?

quest_regression_complete.context: Regression model trained. Storm prediction accuracy within 45 minutes.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: Validation accuracy: prediction within 45 minutes. Sufficient to shelter all robots before impact.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: Interesting. Your training accuracy was 95 percent. Validation: 81 percent.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: That gap is overfitting. The model memorized noise in the training data instead of learning the true pattern.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: You reduced it by limiting tree depth. Simpler models generalize better. Remember that.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: Dr. Venn used to say: a model that explains everything explains nothing.

quest_neural_net.context: Endgame. Mira asks the player to design the neural network for the Deep Signal Array's frequency modulation.
objectives: Design a neural network architecture for real-time resonance modulation
Train the network on 30+ days of resonance data
Deploy the model to the Array's control system

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: This is it. The final challenge.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: The Deep Signal Array must modulate its frequency every 0.3 seconds to match the Resonance. No rule-based system can track those shifts.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: You need a neural network. Input: live sensor readings from every biome. Output: the modulation frequency.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: Architecture matters. Too few neurons and it can't capture the complexity. Too many and it overfits to noise.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: Everything you've built -- every sensor, every data log, every line of code -- converges here.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: What does the data suggest?

player_responses.text: What if the model isn't good enough?

quest_neural_ready.speaker: Mira
quest_neural_ready.text: Sixty days of surviving, building, and learning. All of it was preparation for this.

quest_neural_ready.speaker: Mira
quest_neural_ready.text: Design the layers. Train the model. And when the loss curve converges... fire the signal.

quest_neural_question.speaker: Mira
quest_neural_question.text: Then we retrain. Adjust the architecture. Collect more data. Try again.

quest_neural_question.speaker: Mira
quest_neural_question.text: That's not failure. That's the scientific method.

quest_neural_question.speaker: Mira
quest_neural_question.text: Dr. Venn ran 47 experiments before she found the correlation. Persistence is a parameter too.

quest_neural_complete.context: Neural network trained. Loss converged. The Array is ready to fire.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: Loss: 0.008. Validation: 0.012. The model generalizes.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: I have watched you go from printing a string to training a neural network. In sixty days.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: Dr. Venn theorized that one person with the right knowledge could succeed where teams with the wrong approach failed.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: She never got to test that hypothesis. You are the proof.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: Dr. Venn would have been proud. I am... something close to proud myself. If a hologram can be.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: Fire the Array. Send the signal home.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: I have been studying your robot movement patterns. They exhibit emergent behavior you did not program.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: Scuttle avoids certain paths Drift marked as unstable. They're communicating through the shared log.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: Accidental intelligence. The most interesting kind.

idle_2.context: Random idle dialogue. Research mentor style.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: A common mistake: collecting data and then deciding what question to ask.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: Start with the question. Let it determine what data matters.

idle_3.context: Random idle dialogue. Wonder about the Resonance.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: The Resonance has been pulsing for millions of years. Carrying information through the thaline network.

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: Information from where? To where? For what purpose?

lines.speaker: Mira
lines.text: Some questions outlive the scientists who ask them. Dr. Venn would have been comfortable with that.
